title: Ensure that the --peer-cert-file and --peer-key-file arguments are set as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --peer-cert-file and --peer-key-file arguments are set as appropriate
input_row.description: etcd should be configured to make use of TLS encryption for peer connections.
input_row.rationale: etcd is a highly-available key value store used by Kubernetes deployments for persistent storage of all of its REST API objects. These objects are sensitive in nature and should be encrypted in transit and also amongst peers in the etcd clusters.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the etcd server node: ps -ef | grep etcd Verify that the --peer-cert-file and --peer-key-file arguments are set as appropriate. Note: This recommendation is applicable only for etcd clusters. If you are using only one etcd server in your environment then this recommendation is not applicable.
input_row.remediation: Follow the etcd service documentation and configure peer TLS encryption as appropriate for your etcd cluster. Impact: etcd cluster peers would need to set up TLS for their communication. Default Value: Note: This recommendation is applicable only for etcd clusters. If you are using only one etcd server in your environment then this recommendation is not applicable. By default, peer communication over TLS is not configured.   119 | P a g e References: 1. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/security.html 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/etcd/
input_row.impact: etcd cluster peers would need to set up TLS for their communication. Default Value: Note: This recommendation is applicable only for etcd clusters. If you are using only one etcd server in your environment then this recommendation is not applicable. By default, peer communication over TLS is not configured.   119 | P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control requires verification that the etcd process is started with the --peer-cert-file and --peer-key-file arguments, which are OS-level process flags. etcd is not managed as a standard Kubernetes API object, and these flags are not exposed via the Kubernetes API or kubectl. The audit procedure explicitly instructs to run a process listing (ps -ef | grep etcd) on the etcd server node, which requires host-level access outside the scope of Kubernetes APIs. Therefore, this check cannot be fully automated via Kubernetes-native interfaces and requires manual/host-level inspection.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into each etcd server node in your Kubernetes cluster.
2. Run the following command to list the etcd process and its arguments:
   ```
   ps -ef | grep etcd
   ```
3. Examine the process arguments to verify that both --peer-cert-file and --peer-key-file are present and set to valid certificate and key file paths.
4. Confirm that the certificate and key files referenced actually exist on the filesystem and are readable by the etcd process.
5. If you are running a single etcd server (not a cluster), this control is not applicable.
6. Document your findings for compliance evidence.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES 1.6 BENCHMARK V1.0.0 ARCHIVE
